questions.question: Explain the difference between static and instance members in C++ (3 marks)
expectedPoints: Static members are shared across all objects, instance members are unique per object
Static members exist without object creation, instance members require object instantiation
Static functions cannot access non-static members directly

questions.question: Describe the proper way to declare, define, and use static data members and static member functions with examples (8 marks)
expectedPoints: Declaration inside class using static keyword
Definition outside class using scope resolution
Example of static data member for object counting
